one_liner: GitHub's REST/GraphQL APIs offer broad, free self-serve programmatic access to repos, issues, and workflows via token or OAuth authentication, with an existing official MCP server making agent integration buildable today.

auth_methods.notes: Personal access tokens (fine-grained or classic) or GITHUB_TOKEN in Actions, sent as Authorization: Bearer/token header.
GitHub Apps and OAuth Apps support OAuth2 flows for delegated access.
Some OAuth app endpoints require basic auth using client ID/secret.

self_serve_notes: Free plan accounts can self-generate personal access tokens or register GitHub/OAuth Apps with no payment required; usage subject to rate limits (5,000 req/hr authenticated).

api_surface.notes: Extensive REST API covering repos, issues, PRs, actions, orgs, etc. GraphQL API also available separately.

evidence.supports: Describes token-based auth via Authorization header, personal access tokens, GitHub Apps, GITHUB_TOKEN.
Confirms free self-serve access with rate limits (5000 req/hr authenticated).
Confirms free tier available for all accounts including API access.
